subject: LU-2469 tests: use MDS/OSTDEV and MDS/OSTDEVBASE with zfs

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

messages.message:
Patch Set 1:

Build Started http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/12025/

Patch Set 1: Verified

Build Successful 
 
http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/12025/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Nathaniel, notwithstanding the typo in "envdefinitions", my understanding is that using "envdefinitions=FSTYPE=zfs" is _not_ going to work properly with autotest, because the autotest configuration itself (creating LVM devices, etc) needs to be done before the tests are started.

You should instead use the proper Test-Parameters: settings for the filesystem types, per http://wiki.whamcloud.com/display/PUB/Changing+Test+Parameters+with+Gerrit+Commit+Messages:

 Test-Parameters: mdtfilesystemtype=zfs ostfilesystemtype=zfs

and this will internally set the appropriate *FSTYPE environment variables.  The comment in the wiki page for "envdefinitions" explicitly warns against using it for "FSTYPE".
Uploaded patch set 2.

Patch Set 4:

MDSDEV*, OSTDEV*, etc. are supposed to store block devices (or files for loop devices); xxxdevname()s should print block devices for ldiskfs and dataset for zfs, while xxxvdevname()s should print nothing for ldiskfs and block devices for zfs.  These were working before the DNE tests landings.

Patch Set 4:

This is what my patch tries to accomplish.  It also allows users to set data set name in the case of ZFS backing.

Patch Set 9:

By the way, I find it helps, when making a patch like this, if I temporarily change llmount.sh to call all *devname()s and *vdevname()s for all targets and test with a few configurations, especially the standalone MGS cases that Autotest does not cover.  TF is hard to get right and doing that saved me quite a few times.

parents.subject: LU-1538 tests: run sanity-lfsck instead of lfsck

commit.subject: LU-2469 tests: use MDS/OSTDEV and MDS/OSTDEVBASE with zfs
commit.message:
LU-2469 tests: use MDS/OSTDEV and MDS/OSTDEVBASE with zfs

Add support for using MDSDEVn, OSTDEVn, MDSDEVBASE and OSTDEVBASE
variables when testing with zfs.
Add *ZFSDEVn variable for specifying ZFS format devices.
Remove use of *DEVn use outside of test-framework.sh

MDSDEV et al. specify physical device formatted.

Formerly Change-Id I89115ce935294cdbbd6620fec3f9dc3eb76f8960

Test-Parameters: mdsfilesystemtype=zfs ostfilesystemtype=zfs  mdtfilesystemtype=zfs testlist=sanity
Signed-off-by: Nathaniel Clark <nathaniel.l.clark@intel.com>
Change-Id: If6506995d9d61f9c97827f45071f0d317c7a09f9
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/5016
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Jian Yu <jian.yu@intel.com>

Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
